zh.lifeSummary: Jan Jan Wouter !! Jordaan Alexander (RR)(BG) van Gent Vilain是Jean II Vilain van Bouchout的兒子，於大約1330年出生。他和Margaretha Rutger Rutger van Gent de Gand Briseteste Huysse至少有1個兒子。他去世於1396年之前。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Jan Jan Wouter !! Jordaan Alexander (RR)(BG) van Gent Vilain是Jean II Vilain van Bouchout的儿子，于大约1330年出生。他和Margaretha Rutger Rutger van Gent de Gand Briseteste Huysse至少有1个儿子。他去世于1396年之前。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Gent
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northern) and French: nickname from Old French gent, Middle English gent(e) ‘high born, noble’, hence ‘noble of character or conduct, courteous’, also ‘nice, friendly’.
English: habitational name from Ghent (Gent in Dutch), a city in Flanders, Belgium. Compare Ghent .
German: from a short form of an ancient Germanic personal name formed with gand ‘spell’, such as Gandolf.

variants.name: Jent
meanings: English: variant of   Gent  .

variants.name: Ghent
meanings: English: habitational name from Ghent (Gent in Dutch), a city in Flanders, Belgium. Compare Gent .

variants.name: Gentes
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Henri, Lucien, Marcel, Normand.
French (Gentès): probably a nickname derived from Old French gent ‘high born, noble’, also ‘nice, friendly’.
German: patronymic from the personal name   Gent  .

variants.name: Genther
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Fritz, Otto.
German:
variant of   Gent  .

variants.name: Genthe
meanings: German: variant of Gente (see Gent ), or a habitational name from Gentha in Saxony-Anhalt, east of Wittenberg.

nameMeanings.name: Vilain
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English:  see  Villain  .

variants.name: Villain
meanings: Norman, English: status name from Anglo-Norman French villein, vilein ‘serf, bondman, servile tenant’, denoting an occupier or cultivator of land who was entirely subject to a lord or was attached to a manor.

nameMeanings.name: Jan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

variants.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

nameMeanings.name: Jan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

variants.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

nameMeanings.name: Wouter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   Walther .

variants.name: Walther
meanings: from wald ‘rule’ + heri ‘army’; name of the famous medieval minnesinger Walther von der Vogelweide ( c.1170– c.1230 ).

nameMeanings.name: Jordaan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Feminine form of   Jordan .

nameMeanings.name: Alexander
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

en.lifeSummary: Jan Jan Wouter !! Jordaan Alexander (RR)(BG) van Gent Vilain was born about 1330 as the son of Jean II Vilain van Bouchout. He had at least 1 son with Margaretha Rutger Rutger van Gent de Gand Briseteste Huysse. He died before 1396.

fr.lifeSummary: Jan Jan Wouter !! Jordaan Alexander (RR)(BG) van Gent Vilain est né environ 1330, fils de Jean II Vilain van Bouchout. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Margaretha Rutger Rutger van Gent de Gand Briseteste Huysse. Il est décédé avant 1396.

de.lifeSummary: Jan Jan Wouter !! Jordaan Alexander (RR)(BG) van Gent Vilain wurde um 1330, als Sohn von Jean II Vilain van Bouchout geboren. Er hatte mit Margaretha Rutger Rutger van Gent de Gand Briseteste Huysse mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb vor 1396.

it.lifeSummary: Jan Jan Wouter !! Jordaan Alexander (RR)(BG) van Gent Vilain è nato il circa 1330 come figlio di Jean II Vilain van Bouchout. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Margaretha Rutger Rutger van Gent de Gand Briseteste Huysse. Morì prima del 1396.

ja.lifeSummary: Jan Jan Wouter !! Jordaan Alexander (RR)(BG) van Gent Vilainは約1330年にJean II Vilain van Bouchoutの息子として生まれた。彼はMargaretha Rutger Rutger van Gent de Gand Briseteste Huysseとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1396年以前に死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jan Jan Wouter !! Jordaan Alexander (RR)(BG) van Gent Vilain(은)는 대략 1330년 Jean II Vilain van Bouchout의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Margaretha Rutger Rutger van Gent de Gand Briseteste Huysse(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1396년 이전 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Jan Jan Wouter !! Jordaan Alexander (RR)(BG) van Gent Vilain nasceu aproximadamente 1330 filho de Jean II Vilain van Bouchout. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Margaretha Rutger Rutger van Gent de Gand Briseteste Huysse. Ele faleceu antes 1396.

ru.lifeSummary: Jan Jan Wouter !! Jordaan Alexander (RR)(BG) van Gent Vilain родился около 1330 г. Его отца звали Jean II Vilain van Bouchout. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Margaretha Rutger Rutger van Gent de Gand Briseteste Huysse. Он умер перед 1396 г.
